step_by_step_thinking:
The child swallowed a small quantity of concentrated fluoride solution, so the most appropriate initial step would involve neutralizing the ingested fluoride to mitigate any toxic effects. Drinking large volumes of water might dilute the fluoride too quickly without providing adequate binding agents to precipitate harmful ions. Milk or another calcium-rich beverage acts effectively as a buffering mechanism, helping to bind excess fluoride and reducing its absorption into the bloodstream.
\boxed{C} Based on the information regarding the efficacy of calcium-binding substances in mitigating fluoride toxicity, having the child drink milk or another calcium-containing liquid is likely the best immediate response to minimize harm from ingesting a fluoride solution.
